items.prompt: What still anchors advanced collection pipelines according to this part?
choices: Collecting as much data as possible regardless of use
Gathering the right evidence for a concrete decision or action
Replacing all surveys with sensors immediately
items.explain: Example 9.1 and the section opener emphasize decision-focused collection, not data for its own sake.

items.prompt: Traditional collection often fails on scalability, reliability, and cost—motivating advanced techniques.
items.explain: Section 9.1.1 frames those three limits as the practical motivation for the chapter.

items.prompt: Example 9.2 (paper surveys) mainly illustrates which failure mode?
choices: Scalability limits as sample size grows
items.explain: Paper survey logistics become exponentially harder as the sample grows.

items.prompt: Why do wearables (Example 9.5) motivate advanced continuous collection?
items.rubric: Mention continuous streams, higher temporal resolution, or that clinic visits/paper records cannot match that resolution.
items.sample_answer: Wearables generate continuous heart-rate, activity, and sleep streams that periodic clinic visits and paper records cannot capture at the same temporal resolution.

items.prompt: Heterogeneous modern sources often require continuous rather than one-shot collection.
items.explain: Section 9.1.2 stresses velocity and continuous ingestion across sensors, social, logs, and multimedia.
